title: podman-remote: images -f: does not actually filter
body:
Usual setup for podman system service, then
```console
$ ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK pull alpine     ! just to get some images loaded
a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e
$ ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK pull busybox
1c35c441208254cb7c3844ba95a96485388cef9ccc0646d562c7fc026e04c807

$ ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK images -f label=foo=bar
REPOSITORY                 TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
docker.io/library/busybox  latest  1c35c4412082  8 days ago   1.44 MB
docker.io/library/alpine   latest  a24bb4013296  12 days ago  5.85 MB
```
Expected output: just a header, no images. (`./bin/podman images -f label=foo=bar` works as expected)
